content:
✨ GenRecon | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To reconstruct detailed and editable 3D meshes of indoor scenes from multiple 2D images by combining generative modeling with scene decomposition.
✨ Key Features: • High-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view images • Integration of strong generative 3D priors (e.g., Trellis.2) • Scene reconstruction via spatially-localized, overlapping chunks
📖 Summary: GenRecon is a Python-based framework for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view RGB images. It leverages strong generative 3D priors, specifically Trellis.2, by conditioning the generation process on localized scene chunks to achieve scene-scale reconstruction. The approach enables faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions of indoor environments, outperforming existing methods.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
